content:
# EHO Inspection Quick-Ready Checklist

**Tool ID:** T018-01
**Block:** B018 (EHO Inspection Readiness)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 10 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

VMVT inspectors arrive unannounced. This is the 10-minute checklist you run the moment you hear "inspector" -- or better yet, every morning so you never need to panic.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

One-page rapid readiness checklist the manager can run through in 10 minutes to confirm the cafe is inspection-ready: documentation, temperatures, cleaning, storage, hygiene, and staff standards.

This tool supports **EHO Inspection Readiness** (B018) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager (or Shift Lead if Manager is off-site)
**Time Required:** 10 minutes
**Frequency:** Weekly as routine. Immediately when an inspector arrives or is expected.

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (laminate it and keep behind the counter)
- Probe thermometer
- Access to food safety folder

**Steps:**
1. Start at the front door and work through in order -- do not jump around
2. Mark each item: READY (green) or NOT READY (red)
3. For NOT READY items, fix immediately if possible (under 2 minutes)
4. If a fix takes longer, assign it to a team member with a time limit
5. Brief the team: "We are inspection-ready" or "These 3 things need fixing now"

---

## Checklist

### DOCUMENTATION -- Can You Produce These in Under 2 Minutes?

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-----|
| 1 | Food business registration (VMVT) | R | NR | |
| 2 | HACCP / food safety management system | R | NR | |
| 3 | Allergen matrix matching current menu | R | NR | |
| 4 | Temperature logs -- last 7 days complete, no gaps | R | NR | |
| 5 | Cleaning records -- last 7 days complete | R | NR | |
| 6 | Staff food hygiene certificates for all on shift today | R | NR | |
| 7 | Pest control contract and latest report | R | NR | |
| 8 | Previous inspection report and completed action plan | R | NR | |

### TEMPERATURES -- Check Right Now

| # | Item | Reading | Target | Ready | Not Ready | Fix |
|---|------|---------|--------|-------|-----------|-----|
| 9 | Fridge 1 temperature | _____ deg C | 1-5 deg C | R | NR | |
| 10 | Fridge 2 temperature (if applicable) | _____ deg C | 1-5 deg C | R | NR | |
| 11 | Freezer temperature | _____ deg C | -18 deg C or below | R | NR | |
| 12 | Display fridge / cake counter | _____ deg C | below 5 deg C | R | NR | |
| 13 | Hot-hold unit (if in use) | _____ deg C | 63 deg C+ | R | NR | |
| 14 | Probe thermometer calibrated? (ice water = 0 +/- 1 deg C) | _____ deg C | 0 deg C | R | NR | |

### STORAGE -- Walk the Fridges and Dry Store

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-----|
| 15 | All food covered, labelled, and date-coded | R | NR | |
| 16 | Raw below cooked in every fridge | R | NR | |
| 17 | No out-of-date items anywhere (check milk, sandwiches, cakes) | R | NR | |
| 18 | Dry store: items off floor, shelves clean, no pest evidence | R | NR | |
| 19 | Chemicals stored separately from food (locked cupboard ideal) | R | NR | |

### CLEANING -- Look at These Specific Spots

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-----|
| 20 | Handwash sink: soap, paper towels, warm water, clear of clutter | R | NR | |
| 21 | Food prep surfaces clean and sanitised | R | NR | |
| 22 | Floor behind equipment (grinder, espresso machine, under fridge) | R | NR | |
| 23 | Waste bins: lined, lidded, not overflowing | R | NR | |
| 24 | Dishwasher / glasswasher clean, drain clear | R | NR | |
| 25 | Toilet facilities: clean, stocked, hand-wash sign displayed | R | NR | |

### STAFF -- Ask and Observe

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-----|
| 26 | All staff in clean uniform/apron, hair tied back | R | NR | |
| 27 | No watches, bracelets, or rings being worn in food areas | R | NR | |
| 28 | Ask 1 team member: "Name 3 allergens" -- can they? | R | NR | |
| 29 | Ask 1 team member: "When must you wash your hands?" -- do they know? | R | NR | |
| 30 | Any staff member visibly unwell? (should not be handling food) | R | NR | |

### STRUCTURE -- Quick Visual Scan

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-----|
| 31 | Walls and ceilings: no flaking paint, mould, or damage | R | NR | |
| 32 | Lighting adequate in kitchen/prep areas | R | NR | |
| 33 | Ventilation / extraction working (no grease build-up) | R | NR | |
| 34 | No evidence of pests (droppings, gnaw marks, dead insects) | R | NR | |

### Readiness Score

| Category | Items | Ready | Not Ready |
|----------|-------|-------|-----------|
| Documentation | 8 | | |
| Temperatures | 6 | | |
| Storage | 5 | | |
| Cleaning | 6 | | |
| Staff | 5 | | |
| Structure | 4 | | |
| **TOTAL** | **34** | | |

**Readiness verdict:**
- 32-34 Ready: Inspection-confident. Maintain this standard.
- 28-31 Ready: Minor fixes needed. Address today.
- 24-27 Ready: Significant gaps. Action plan within 24 hours.
- Below 24: Not ready. Escalate to Owner immediately. Intensive corrective action.

**Completion:**
- Date: _______________
- Time: _______________
- Completed by: _______________
- Overall: _____ / 34 Ready

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Documentation Folder Cannot Be Found

**Why it happens:** Folder was moved during a deep clean, taken home, or never had a fixed location. Inspector asks for HACCP plan and nobody knows where it is.
**How to prevent:** Designate one fixed location for the food safety folder. Label the shelf. Never remove it from the premises.
**If it is already happening:** Create a backup digital copy (photos of all documents) stored on a shared drive or tablet behind the counter.

### Risk 2: Temperature Log Gaps From This Morning

**Why it happens:** Opening barista forgot to log temperatures. Inspector arrives at 10:00 and the log shows nothing for today.
**How to prevent:** Temperature logging is step 3 on the opening checklist (T092-01). It happens before the espresso machine warms up.
**If it is already happening:** Log temperatures now. Add a note: "Recorded at [time] -- delayed from opening." Honest documentation is better than no documentation.

### Risk 3: Staff Cannot Answer Basic Food Safety Questions

**Why it happens:** Training was a one-off event months ago. No refreshers. Staff remember the certificate but not the content.
**How to prevent:** Run a 5-minute allergen quiz at one team meeting per month. Use T019-04 competency assessment quarterly.
**If it is already happening:** Brief the team right now on the 3 most common inspector questions: allergens, handwashing triggers, and temperature danger zone (5-63 deg C).

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add items for pour-over station hygiene and single-origin traceability documents.
**Chain / Franchise:** Cross-reference with your brand pre-audit checklist. This tool covers regulatory basics -- your brand may require more.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Remove Structure section. Add vehicle/stall hygiene and water supply checks.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add display case temperature, proving area cleanliness, and decorated cake allergen labelling.

**Making it yours:**
- Laminate this checklist and keep it behind the POS or in the office
- Run it every Monday morning as a habit, not just when inspectors are expected
- Rotate who does the check -- it trains the whole team
- Celebrate 34/34 weeks with the team -- make readiness a source of pride

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T018-02 -- Document Locator Sheet (L2, template)
- T018-03 -- EHO Inspection SOP (L2, SOP)
- T018-04 -- Post-Inspection Action Plan Template (L2, template)
- T018-05 -- FHRS Rating History Tracker (L3, tracker)

**Related blocks:**
- B016
- B017

**Parent block:** B018 -- EHO Inspection Readiness
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
